Market Analysis: • Coherent Corp. has staged a 57.5% V-shaped recovery from its April low but faces major resistance at the $83.45–$83.53 zone, where the Bollinger upper band and 200-day SMA converge.
• The MACD has delivered a textbook bullish crossover and is at its highest level in the dataset, while the 50-day SMA has just inflected upward, suggesting nascent medium-term trend improvement.

• Despite strong momentum, the rally lacks volume confirmation, the death cross persists, and RSI briefly touched overbought, favoring a pullback toward the 10 EMA near $74 before the next leg higher.
Social Sentiment: • Coherent Corp. reported a record Q3 FY2025 beat and raise, driven by 45% YoY growth in AI datacenter networking.
• Analysts are nearly universally bullish, with price targets ranging from $95 to $110 following the earnings release.
• Insider sales occurred via pre-arranged 10b5-1 plans, but the absence of insider buying tempers bullish conviction.
News Analysis: • Coherent’s Q3 revenue and EPS surged 24% and 141% YoY, driven by AI optical networking.
• The U.S.-China tariff truce eases cost pressures, while higher-for-longer rates and oil above $100/bbl pose risks.
• Consensus price target near $94 implies ~34% upside from $70, with risk/reward favorable for medium-term positions.
Fundamentals: • Coherent executed a strong post-merger turnaround, with accelerating revenue growth (23.4% YoY) and three consecutive profitable quarters.
• The company aggressively deleveraged, cutting its debt-to-equity ratio from 62.1% to 32.1% and building a strong liquidity position with a 3.05x current ratio.
• Despite a high trailing P/E of 185.9x, a PEG ratio of 0.92 and 76% analyst buy ratings suggest the stock remains undervalued relative to its growth trajectory.
Final Decision: • Analyst sides with the bearish case, citing unfavorable near-term risk/reward at $78.
• Key concerns include extreme valuation (P/E 185x), negative free cash flow, lack of insider buying, and bearish technical signals.
• Recommended action is to trim positions by 30–50%, set a hard stop at $69.50, and wait for clarity after the May 28 Analyst Day.
